metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (as of #599 sidecar reader); driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

This is the MOTIVATING cell for the scenario (#599): kiro-cli onboarded its whole column (#590) with model and context utilization invisible — both live only in the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar, never in the tailed JSONL — and nothing before section 5 forced the question, so the gap first surfaced as a user bug report (live session 0217167a-2112-48b1-a745-83232b028211 showing neither). The 1.8 row exists so every future column confronts model/context visibility at first contact.

## agent_supports: yes

kiro's own TUI footer renders exactly these two metrics live (`Kiro · auto · ◔ 1%` — model label + context-fill gauge), and the sidecar persists them per completed turn: `session_state.rts_model_state = {model_info: {model_id, context_window_tokens}, context_usage_percentage}` (live 2.6.0: model_id="auto", context_window_tokens=200000, context_usage_percentage=1.86). "auto" is kiro's unpinned routing label — it is what the agent itself displays, which the scenario acceptance explicitly admits.

## daemon_capability: full (as of #599; was incapable in the v1 adapter)

The #599 sidecar reader closes the gap end to end: the tailer injects the transcript path via tailer.TranscriptPathAware at construction; on every text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done) the parser token-walks the sidecar to session_state.rts_model_state — skipping the potentially-huge conversation_metadata — and sets ModelName ("auto" passes NormalizeModelName unchanged), ContextWindow (from context_window_tokens, so capacity-table lookup is never needed), and a derived Tokens.Total = pct/100 × window so ComputeContextUtilization (total/window) reproduces kiro's own percentage exactly (raw input/output_token_count are ZERO in the sidecar — credits, not tokens; token ACCOUNTING remains frozen at 5.1).

Live-verified 2026-06-05 (kiro-cli 2.6.0, dev daemon 0.4.8+6d2b11a.dirty): websocket push for the probe session carried metrics {model_name: "auto", context_window: 200000, context_utilization_percentage: 1.24, pressure_level: "safe"} — and the percentage matched the CURRENT turn's sidecar value, i.e. kiro flushes the sidecar before/with the JSONL turn flush (no one-turn staleness observed).

## driver_capability: ready

One trivial prompt + wait_turn — both long-ported primitives (DRIVE_ELICITS includes send and wait_turn). Default launch (`kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools`), no model pinning (the DEFAULT-visibility check is the point; the pinned-model variant is 5.2).

## Recording note

Replay reproduction requires the metadata sidecar staged NEXT TO the fixture transcript: the replay engine copies <base>.json to <tmpdir>/transcript.json (engine.go, #599), so the recording curation must capture the live <uuid>.json alongside transcript.jsonl as transcript.json. Without it, replay runs sidecar-less and the model/context assertions fail.

sources.note: applySidecarMetrics: on turn_done sets ModelName/ContextWindow from rts_model_state and derives Tokens.Total = pct/100 × window so ComputeContextUtilization reproduces kiro's own percentage; path injected via SetTranscriptPath (tailer.TranscriptPathAware).
readSidecarModelState: token-walks <base>.json → session_state → rts_model_state, decoding only that subobject and skipping the potentially-huge conversation_metadata (transcript.SkipJSONValue).
TranscriptPathAware optional parser interface; NewTranscriptTailer injects the path at construction (both the live cache path and the replay engine construct through it).
newReplayer stages the source transcript's <base>.json sidecar as <tmpdir>/transcript.json so sidecar-reading parsers see the same fields during replay as live.

Websocket push frame for probe session a43c466a: metrics {model_name: auto, context_window: 200000, context_utilization_percentage: 1.24, pressure_level: safe}; percentage matched the current turn's sidecar (no staleness).

The user-reported invisible session (#599): session_state.rts_model_state = {model_info: {model_id: auto, context_window_tokens: 200000}, context_usage_percentage: 1.86} — the data was on disk all along, unread by the v1 adapter.

caveats: model_name is "auto" unless the user pins a model — that is kiro's own routing label (the TUI footer shows the same), and the scenario acceptance explicitly admits an agent-displayed routing alias. The pinned-model variant is 5.2.

Context utilization is DERIVED from the sidecar's context_usage_percentage (Tokens.Total = pct/100 × window): the sidecar's raw input/output_token_count are zero (credits-only metering), so token ACCOUNTING (5.1) remains frozen — this cell asserts display, not accounting.
capacity has no pricing for "auto" → cost stays 0 for unpinned sessions (not asserted by this scenario).
Replay needs the sidecar captured as transcript.json next to transcript.jsonl in the fixture (engine stages <base>.json); recordings made before #599 lack it and would replay sidecar-less.

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH and logged in (subscription login covers everything; no API key needed)
irrlichd is recording (isolated IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_HOME coexist mode, port 7838)
fresh cwd so the cwd-based PID match is unambiguous
setup: Driver launches `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` in a fresh cwd (default launch — NO model pinning; default visibility is the point of this cell)
Assertions on model_name/context_window/context_utilization_percentage are checked AFTER the first turn_done (the sidecar read fires on the text-only AssistantMessage)
Curation must copy the live <uuid>.json sidecar into the cell as transcript.json next to transcript.jsonl so replay reproduces the metrics

script.text: What is 6 times 7? Answer with just the number.

verify: after the first turn_done, the session push carries metrics.model_name = "auto" (non-empty, not unknown)
metrics.context_window = 200000 (from the sidecar rts_model_state, no capacity lookup)
metrics.context_utilization_percentage > 0 and equal to the sidecar context_usage_percentage
final session state is ready; topology ready → working → ready
staged fixture contains transcript.json (the metadata sidecar) next to transcript.jsonl
